attacking_style: Early breakaway specialist who attacks from the break rather than the peloton, capable of solo efforts in final kilometers against elite chase groups on mountain stages.
team_tactics: Operates with freedom to join early breakaways; minimal visible team setup required as tactics rely on individual initiative from the break.
terrain_preference: Mountain stages with sustained climbs where breakaway specialists can hold off GC riders; demonstrates ability to power through headwinds on exposed finishes.
vulnerability: Limited data prevents clear identification of weaknesses; likely vulnerable when GC teams commit to full chase or on explosive short climbs favoring pure climbers.
key_patterns: Multiple Giro stage wins from breakaways, indicating consistent execution of early-move tactics.
Sustained power in adverse conditions: held off 20-rider GC group in headwinds.
Climbing ability exceeds typical breakaway specialist profile, enabling mountain stage victories.
form_trajectory: positive: Multiple stage wins at same Giro demonstrates excellent form and ability to repeat success
